Here's What It Signals.","content":"May 13, 2026 — Berkshire spent about $234 million on buybacks during the first quarter of 2026. about 0.02% of Berkshire's current market cap of $1 trillion.
Berkshire Hathaway (NYSE: BRKA)(NYSE: BRKB) spent about $78 billion on stock buybacks from 2018 through 2024.
However, we recently learned that Berkshire completed its first buybacks in nearly two years, with recent regulatory filings showing that Berkshire spent about $234 million on buybacks during the first quarter of 2026.
We have no idea whether CEO Greg Abel will (or has already) buy back more stock in the second quarter, which is underway now.
Instead, Berkshire is allowed to buy back stock at any time when the CEO (Abel) and chairman of the board (legendary investor Warren Buffett) agree the stock is trading for a substantial discount to the intrinsic value of the shares.
Berkshire Hathaway's stock traded as low as 1.45 times book value in early March, a significant discount to the 1.6x-1.8x multiples it traded for during much of the 21-month period where no buybacks took place.

Berkshire repurchases Class A and Class B shares when it believes the market price is below its intrinsic value, conservatively determined.
Soliciting material pursuant to Rule 14a-12 under the Exchange Act (17 CFR 240.14a-12)
Shares may be repurchased in open-market repurchases or in privately negotiated transactions, including pursuant to trading plans that may be adopted in accordance with Rule 10b5-1 of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, as amended.
The timing and total amount of stock repurchases will depend on the market prices of our Class A and Class B common shares, market conditions, and other relevant factors.
Pursuant to the requirements of the Securities Exchange Act of 1934, the Registrant has duly caused this report to be signed on its behalf by the undersigned hereunto duly authorized."},{"url":"https://www.investing.com/news/sec-filings/berkshire-hathaway-begins-share-repurchases-following-leadership-transition-93CH-4543808","title":"Berkshire Hathaway begins share repurchases following leadership transition By Investing.com","content":"(NYSE:BRK.A, NYSE:BRK.B) disclosed Wednesday that it has started repurchasing shares of its Class A and Class B common stock under its existing repurchase policy."},{"url":"https://www.barrons.com/articles/berkshire-hathaway-buybacks-greg-abel-valuation-6560a5ce","title":"When Will Berkshire Hathaway Resume Stock Buybacks?","content":"Jan 20, 2026 — When Berkshire last repurchased stock in May 2024, it paid close to $627,000 per A share, which was 1.5 times the June 30, 2024 book value.Read more"}]}

Warren Buffett speaks during the Berkshire Hathaway Annual Shareholders Meeting in Omaha, Nebraska on May 3, 2025. Get in on Berkshire action with the upcoming Annual Meeting on May 2, buybacks, and more! Add me on Instagram: michellemarki

In today's video I discussed a little bit of the changes at this year's Berkshire Hathaway Annual Meeting since there's a new CEO, Greg Abel, who took over from Warren Buffett in 2026.

We won't be hearing Warren Buffett answer any questions this year since he won't be up on stage, but other people like Ajit Jain and the leaders of BNSF and NetJets will be up there.

You can tune into the livestream from CNBC on Saturday, May 2nd.

I discussed how Berkshire Hathaway did $225 million in share buybacks in early March 2026, suggesting a potential buy price of Berkshire B stock at around $480 per share. At the time I recorded this video, the BRK-B was trading around $470 per share, so it could be a potential opportunity. Of course this video is not advice, you must make your own investment decisions.

I also like to keep tabs on Berkshire's stock buys and sells. In Buffett's last quarter as the leader in Q4 2025, Berkshire bought stocks Dominos, Chubb, Chevron, Lamar Advertising, and the New York Times.

More stock buys and sells will be revealed around mid-May when the next 13F filing for Berkshire comes out.

While the future of Berkshire will not be the same without the late Charlie Munger nor Warren Buffett, there will still be plenty of learning lessons from Berkshire action this year and beyond!

If you're interested in learning how to take control of your finances and start becoming an investor like Warren Buffett, check out my free PDF guide: https://michellemarki.com/resources/

I look forward to making more investor friends! Please like and subscribe if you learned something or enjoyed my video. Thank you! :)
